pending.body:
finding_summary: `claim_bead_in_br` treats the recovered pending-mutation journal as proof that the retry flushed this task's `update_bead_status(..., "in_progress")`, and returns success without re-reading the bead (`src/cli/project.rs:977-1007`, `src/adapters/br_process.rs:1054-1081`). The journal only records what used to be pending; it is not authoritative about the current local `br` state at retry time. If an operator or another process changed the local bead back to `open` before the retry, `sync_if_dirty_when_beads_healthy()` can flush that newer state while `includes_owned_update_status()` still short-circuits the claim. Ralph will then proceed as if the bead is exclusively claimed even though it is still open.
severity: high
affected_files: ["src/cli/project.rs", "src/adapters/br_process.rs"]
remediation_hint: After a recovered flush, re-query `br show` and confirm the bead is actually `in_progress` before skipping the explicit `br update`; do not rely on the stale pending-mutation record alone.

finding_summary: `sync_if_dirty_when_beads_healthy()` flushes any repo-wide pending mutation it finds (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:1329-1370`), and `claim_bead_in_br()` now calls it before every new claim (`src/cli/project.rs:977-999`). That can publish another in-flight multi-step mutation sequence in the same workspace. A concrete case is proposed-bead creation, which does `create_bead` and only later adds the dependency before syncing (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3261-3299`). If a claim or reconciliation runs between those two steps, it will `br sync --flush-only` the half-finished bead and `sync_flush_locked()` will clear the original recovery markers (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:1241-1250`). A crash before the second mutation then leaves upstream state partially applied with no durable signal that recovery still needs to finish.
severity: high
affected_files: ["src/cli/project.rs", "src/adapters/br_process.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
remediation_hint: Do not auto-flush foreign repo dirtiness at the start of unrelated operations. Either hold the repo lock across multi-step mutation sequences until their own flush completes, or make the pre-claim/pre-recovery path fail closed when the pending-mutation journal contains work owned by another adapter or workflow.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 19 -> Plan and Implement
